title: Signs of subdued demand weighs on oil prices
section_path: Signs of subdued demand weighs on oil prices
section_path_text: Signs of subdued demand weighs on oil prices
summary:
Main
Commodity prices fell across sectors after Trump's US election win. The strong USD reduced investor appetite for precious metals, while uncertainty around Trump's economic and foreign policies weighed on cyclical commodities. By Daniel Hynes
Image reference: Στιγμιότυπο οθόνης 2024-11-11 131030.png | assets/2024-11-11_signs-of-subdued-demand-weighs-on-oil-prices_img_cea3cf84ceb9ceb3cebcceb9cf8ccf84cf85_feccd19e6bc6.png

Market Commentary
Crude oil prices fell late week after hitting a high of USD75/bbl.

children.title: Main
children.summary: Commodity prices fell across sectors after Trump's US election win. The strong USD reduced investor appetite for precious metals, while uncertainty around Trump's economic and foreign policies weighed on cyclical commodities.

children.title: Market Commentary
children.summary: Crude oil prices fell late week after hitting a high of USD75/bbl. Higher than expected US oil inventories and subdued Chinese oil imports outweighed OPEC's decision to delay planned output hikes by a month.

children.title: Chart of the Day
children.summary: US investment in the power grid is expected to rise strong over the next two years, driven not only by renewable energy capacity additions but also rising demand for electricity from battery & semiconductor facilities and data centres. This should support copper and aluminium demand.
